summary: Ukrainian drone strikes targeted Sevastopol, Crimea, and Russian border regions overnight, with the Sevastopol panorama museum building destroyed. Bulgaria announced it will no longer supply weapons to Ukraine, while the Netherlands withdrew from the British Interflex training program. The US called for an immediate comprehensive ceasefire and expressed concern over recent Russian strikes that killed over 20 civilians.
key_points: Ukrainian drones struck Sevastopol, destroying the panorama museum building, and targeted Crimea's energy and logistics infrastructure overnight. (Source: @tass_agency/380004) (Source: @tass_agency/380005) (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)
Over 326 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions including Belgorod, Bryansk, Kaluga, Kursk, Orel, Tula, Moscow region, Crimea, and the Azov and Black Seas. (Source: @tass_agency/380002)
Bulgaria's Defense Minister announced Bulgaria will no longer supply weapons to Ukraine, stating Kiev needs more people, not more weapons. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30795)
The Netherlands is withdrawing from the British Interflex training program for Ukrainian Armed Forces personnel. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30787)
The US called on Russia and Ukraine to immediately agree to a comprehensive ceasefire, stating the war can only end through negotiations. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30779)
Russian forces attacked Kharkov and Chuguiv, with a dozen administrative and production premises hit in Kharkov. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)
The Chongar Bridge to Crimea was attacked shortly after reverse traffic for cars was announced, damaging the bridge again. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)
Ukraine's Ministry of Defense reported Russia fitted Kalibr missiles with cluster warheads for the first time this spring. (Source: @UkraineNow/81796)

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drone strikes targeted Sevastopol, Crimea, and Russian border regions overnight, with the Sevastopol panorama museum building destroyed. Bulgaria announced it will no longer supply weapons to Ukraine, while the Netherlands withdrew from the British Interflex training program. The US called for an immediate comprehensive ceasefire and expressed concern over recent Russian strikes that killed over 20 civilians.

points: Ukrainian forces launched overnight drone strikes targeting Sevastopol and Crimea, hitting energy and logistics infrastructure. The Sevastopol panorama museum building was destroyed in the attack. A new strike UAV called 'Behemoth' was used, claimed to have a range of up to 300 km, speed up to 200 km/h, and combat load up to 75 kg. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780) (Source: @tass_agency/380005)

Ukraine's Ministry of Defense reported that Russia fitted Kalibr missiles with cluster warheads for the first time this spring, similar to those used on Kh-101 missiles. The seeker heads again consist of 80-90% foreign components, suggesting a return to proven schemes after a failed transition to domestic electronics. (Source: @UkraineNow/81796)

Ukrainian drones struck a gas station in the Kherson region where people were queuing for refueling, killing one man. The Chongar Bridge to Crimea was also attacked shortly after authorities announced resumption of reverse car traffic. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Ukrainian Interior Minister Klymenko stated police are being trained to politely treat citizens during mobilization escorts to TCCs, claiming shameful incidents have been minimized. Three army corps officials in Kharkiv region are suspected of extorting 274,000 hryvnias from soldiers for fake combat bonus reports since late 2025. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30799) (Source: @UkraineNow/81815)

The EU Commission proposed banning entry to the EU for all Russian military personnel who participated in the war against Ukraine, as part of the 21st sanctions package. The decision requires unanimous approval by EU member states. (Source: @UkraineNow/81798)

Russian forces attacked Kharkov and Chuguiv, with the city administration confirming a dozen administrative and production premises were hit. Russian forces noted an increase in fire impact on the Kharkiv-Sumy logistics route. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Over 326 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions overnight, including Belgorod, Bryansk, Kaluga, Kursk, Orel, Tula, Moscow region, Crimea, and the Azov and Black Seas. Several drones heading toward Moscow and Sochi were intercepted. (Source: @tass_agency/380002) (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

In the Belgorod region, FPV drone strikes injured multiple civilians: two victims hospitalized in Borisovka, a woman with shrapnel injuries in Glotovo, five civilian women injured in Belovskoe, and a 14-year-old boy injured in Dunayka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Russian forces are advancing in the Sumy region in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, and Nova Sich. In Kupyansk, small groups are infiltrating the southeast of the city with active tactical UAV operations. In Krasnyi Liman, fighting continues in the city center and Liman railway station area. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

The Russian Defense Ministry announced the liberation of Khimik (Molocharka) settlement northeast of Konstantinovka, with fierce battles ongoing on the city's western outskirts. In the Dnepropetrovsk region, Russian troops are penetrating enemy defense lines northwest of Alexandrograd. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Russian forces attacked Kharkov and Chuguiv, hitting a dozen administrative and production premises. Fire impact on the Kharkiv-Sumy logistics route has increased. Multiple drone groups were detected over Kharkiv region heading toward Zlatopil, Balakliya, Pechenihy, and Poltava region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780) (Source: @eRadarrua/96104) (Source: @eRadarrua/96107)

In the Sumy front, Russian assault aircraft are engaged in firefights in Bachevsk in the Shostka district. Russian forces are advancing in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, and Nova Sich in the Sumy region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

In Kupyansk, small Russian groups are infiltrating the southeast of the city while deploying reserves and conducting active tactical UAV operations. In Krasnyi Liman, fighting continues in the city center and around the Liman railway station. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Northeast of Konstantinovka, the Russian Defense Ministry announced liberation of Khimik (Molocharka) settlement. Fierce battles for multi-story buildings are ongoing on the western outskirts with high-intensity combat. In the Dobropillia direction, clashes are reported in the Grishino area. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

In the Dnepropetrovsk region, Russian troops are penetrating enemy defense lines northwest of Alexandrograd, conducting offensive operations in a forested area beyond the Volchya River. Drones were detected heading toward Kryvyi Rih from the north. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780) (Source: @kpszsu/64715)

In the Belgorod region, FPV drone strikes injured multiple civilians across several villages: two hospitalized in Borisovka, a woman with shrapnel injuries in Glotovo, five civilian women injured in Belovskoe, and a 14-year-old boy injured in Dunayka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Russian forces are advancing in the Sumy region in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, and Nova Sich and its surroundings. In the Shostka district, Russian assault aircraft are engaged in firefights in Bachevsk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)
In Kupyansk, small Russian groups are infiltrating the southeast of the city while deploying reserves and conducting active operations with tactical UAVs. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)
Enemy tactical aviation activity was reported on the northeastern, eastern, and southeastern directions, with a threat of aviation strike means for frontline regions. (Source: @kpszsu/64710)

Ukrainian drones struck Sevastopol and Crimea overnight, targeting energy and logistics infrastructure. The Sevastopol panorama museum building was destroyed. A new strike UAV 'Behemoth' was used with claimed range up to 300 km and 75 kg combat load. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780) (Source: @tass_agency/380005)

The Chongar Bridge to Crimea was attacked shortly after authorities announced resumption of reverse car traffic, damaging the bridge again. The attack was part of Ukraine's military operation to blockade the peninsula. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

In the Zaporozhye region, many small settlements in frontline areas face prolonged power outages due to constant Ukrainian attacks. Drones were detected heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the south and guided aviation bomb launches were reported. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780) (Source: @kpszsu/64705) (Source: @kpszsu/64685)

In the Kherson region, Ukrainian forces attacked a gas station where people were queuing for refueling, killing one man. Drones were detected heading toward Kryvyi Rih from Dnipropetrovsk region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780) (Source: @kpszsu/64699)

Over 326 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions overnight, including Belgorod, Bryansk, Kaluga, Kursk, Orel, Tula, Moscow region, Crimea, and the Azov and Black Seas. Several drones heading toward Moscow and Sochi were intercepted. (Source: @tass_agency/380002) (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)
Ukraine used a new strike UAV called 'Behemoth' to attack Crimea, claimed to have a range of up to 300 km, speed up to 200 km/h, and combat load up to 75 kg. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Enemy tactical aviation activity was reported on the northeastern, eastern, and southeastern directions, with a threat of aviation strike means for frontline regions. Guided aviation bomb launches were reported on Zaporizhzhia. (Source: @kpszsu/64710) (Source: @kpszsu/64685)

Multiple drone groups were detected across Kharkiv, Mykolaiv, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia, and Dnipropetrovsk regions heading toward various targets including Zlatopil, Balakliya, Pechenihy, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia, and Kryvyi Rih. (Source: @eRadarrua/96104) (Source: @eRadarrua/96107) (Source: @eRadarrua/96061)

Ukraine's Ministry of Defense reported Russia fitted Kalibr missiles with cluster warheads for the first time this spring. The seeker heads again consist of 80-90% foreign components, suggesting Russia returned to foreign electronics after a failed domestic transition. (Source: @UkraineNow/81796)

key_developments: Ukrainian drone strikes destroyed the Sevastopol panorama museum building and targeted Crimea's energy and logistics infrastructure overnight, using a new 'Behemoth' UAV with claimed 300 km range and 75 kg combat load. (Source: @tass_agency/380005) (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Over 326 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions including Belgorod, Bryansk, Kaluga, Kursk, Orel, Tula, Moscow region, Crimea, and the Azov and Black Seas overnight. (Source: @tass_agency/380002)

Bulgaria's Defense Minister announced Bulgaria will no longer supply weapons to Ukraine, stating Kiev needs more people, not more weapons, and called for a just peace determined by both sides. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30795)
The Netherlands is withdrawing from the British Interflex training program for Ukrainian Armed Forces personnel, according to the Netherlands' Ministry of Defense. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30787)

The US called on Russia and Ukraine to immediately agree to a comprehensive ceasefire at a UN Security Council meeting, stating the war can only end through negotiations and expressing concern over recent Russian strikes killing over 20 civilians. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30779)

Russian forces attacked Kharkov and Chuguiv, hitting a dozen administrative and production premises, and announced liberation of Khimik settlement northeast of Konstantinovka with fierce battles ongoing. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30780)

Ukraine's Ministry of Defense reported Russia fitted Kalibr missiles with cluster warheads for the first time this spring, with seeker heads again consisting of 80-90% foreign components. (Source: @UkraineNow/81796)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict shows signs of escalation with large-scale Ukrainian drone strikes targeting Crimea and Russian border regions, while Russia continues ground advances in Sumy, Kupyansk, and Konstantinovka directions. The destruction of the Sevastopol panorama museum represents a symbolic strike, while the Chongar Bridge attack demonstrates Ukraine's ongoing efforts to blockade Crimea. Russia's use of cluster warheads on Kalibr missiles and continued strikes on civilian infrastructure indicate sustained offensive capabilities. The withdrawal of Bulgaria from weapons supply and the Netherlands from training programs signals potential cracks in Western support cohesion.
sections.regional_response: The US called for an immediate comprehensive ceasefire at the UN Security Council, expressing concern over recent Russian strikes that killed over 20 civilians. Bulgaria announced it will no longer supply weapons to Ukraine, with its Defense Minister calling for a just peace determined by both sides. The Netherlands withdrew from the British Interflex training program. The EU Commission proposed banning entry to the EU for all Russian military personnel involved in the war. Polish Prime Minister Tusk admitted Zelensky's words sometimes anger him, amid ongoing tensions over UPA commemorations. Estonia's President suggested this summer could be a window of opportunity to pressure Russia to negotiate.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukraine's use of the new 'Behemoth' UAV with 300 km range and 75 kg combat load represents a significant capability advancement for striking deep into Crimea. Russia's return to foreign components (80-90%) in Kalibr missile seeker heads suggests domestic electronics failed to meet accuracy requirements. The scale of drone operations is substantial, with over 326 drones reportedly shot down in one night across multiple Russian regions. Bulgaria's decision to halt weapons supplies, combined with the Netherlands' withdrawal from training, may indicate growing fatigue among some Western supporters. The EU Commission's proposal to ban entry for Russian military personnel requires unanimous approval, which may face obstacles from member states like Hungary.
